short: An open-source tool for running open-weight models locally with a simple CLI and API.
definition: Ollama is an open-source tool that packages and runs open-weight models on a personal computer through a simple command line and a local, OpenAI-compatible API. Built on llama.cpp, it handles model downloads, quantized formats, and GPU/CPU execution, so developers can swap a cloud API for a private, offline model without changing their application code.
